pm_decision_extract.reasoning_summary: TSVT surged +85% at open (2025-03-11 09:30 ET) but collapsed 47% intraday by decision timestamp. Fundamental deterioration is severe: Q3 2024 shows -73% net margin, -$10.5M free cash flow, negative ROE/ROA. Analyst sentiment has collapsed from 6 buy-side ratings (Oct 2022) to 1 Buy/3 Hold/1 StrongSell (Mar 2025); Goldman Sachs maintains Sell. The surge lacks corroborating narrative: no news items in PIT-safe window, no SEC 8-K confirmation, no clinical trial data (adapter not wired), and alternative data is polluted (Wikipedia pageviews mapped to rifle cartridge article, not company). Structural absence of sector-expected evidence (SEC filings, clinical trial registry, news corroboration) relative to the public narrative of a biotech company is bear-thesis evidence per §5.17 asymmetric absence rule. The intraday collapse from $4.92 to $2.61 (opening print) suggests the surge dynamic has already exhausted. Macro regime (strict_quality_and_valuation_caution) argues against holding negative-margin biotech at elevated multiples. Risk agent confirms hard-veto pass on all §5.13 conditions. Preponderance of evidence supports short thesis: (i) explicit negative fundamentals, (ii) structural absence of narrative corroboration, (iii) analyst downgrades, (iv) intraday exhaustion, (v) macro headwind. No single dimension is overwhelming, but synthesis across fundamentals, valuation, sentiment, and evidence-of-absence points coherently toward bear thesis. Confidence is medium due to data quality issues (adapter failures, alt-data pollution) and missing news catalyst confirmation, but these gaps do not invalidate the short thesis—they are consistent with a pump narrative lacking hard-to-fake support.

If institutional buying pressure sustains price above $4.00 for 5+ consecutive trading days
If company announces strategic partnership or financing that materially improves cash runway
If subsequent earnings beat consensus and show margin improvement trajectory

For an opening-window decision, this creates a logical tension: any news published after market open (09:30) is dropped as post-cutoff, yet the price action at 09:30 may have been driven by pre-market news or after-hours announcements. Analyst sentiment deteriorated from 6 buy-side (Oct 2022) to 1 Buy/3 Hold/1 StrongSell (Mar 2025). No news catalyst, no SEC 8-K confirmation, no alt-data corroboration. Structural absence of expected filings and narrative evidence supports bear thesis. Macro regime (strict quality caution) argues against holding. Short thesis valid; entry at 0.5% per §4.8 fixed sizing.

§2.9-§2.12 hard never-short filter: TSVT is biotech (not in never-short list); no reverse-split history visible; no pump-related corporate action flagged
Bear-thesis sufficient conditions (any ONE supports short, not all required):
(i) Surge dynamic showing exhaustion: YES—intraday collapse from $4.92 to $2.61 (opening print) by decision timestamp; volume profile not fully visible but price action shows exhaustion

(ii) Public narrative inconsistent with verifiable evidence: YES—no news catalyst within PIT-safe cutoff; 20 items dropped post-cutoff; no SEC 8-K confirmation; no clinical trial data (adapter not wired); Wikipedia pageviews polluted (unrelated article)

(iii) Structural absence relative to sector-expected evidence: YES—biotech company should produce clinical trial registry rows, FDA filing footprints, SEC filing activity; all absent or adapter-failed
(iv) Negative or zero fundamental ratios combined with extreme price action: YES—net margin -73.39%, FCF -$10.5M, ROE -8.96%, ROA -3.97%; combined with +85% gap and 47% intraday collapse
(v) Reverse-split / pump-related corporate action history: NOT VISIBLE in packet, but not required
Preponderance-of-evidence synthesis: (i), (ii), (iii), (iv) all present and coherent; bear thesis is well-supported
Macro regime (Overheat/Moderately Restrictive) supports strict quality discipline; negative-margin biotech at elevated multiples is disfavored
ADaS lagged (SUNE, 2025-03-07): high confidence_dispersion suggests framework uncertainty on similar surge-short candidates; PM retains full discretion; no override of this decision
DECISION: short at §4.8 fixed 0.5% entry sizing

(2) Analyst sentiment has collapsed (1 Buy vs 6 buy-side in Oct 2022; Goldman Sachs Sell). (3) Surge dynamic shows exhaustion (intraday collapse from $4.92 to $2.61 by 09:30 ET). (4) Narrative lacks corroboration (no news within cutoff, no SEC 8-K, no clinical trial data, Wikipedia pageviews polluted). (5) Structural absence of sector-expected evidence (SEC filings failed, clinical adapter not wired, news window empty) is bear-thesis evidence per §5.17 asymmetric absence rule. (6) Macro regime (strict quality caution) disfavors negative-margin biotech. All dimensions point coherently toward bear thesis. Confidence is medium due to data quality issues (adapter failures, alt-data pollution) and missing news catalyst confirmation, but these gaps do not invalidate the short thesis—they are consistent with a pump narrative lacking hard-to-fake support.

narrative_event.reasoning_summary: TSVT exhibits a 5-day return of +12.90% with relative volume 2.08x the 20-day average, and an intraday open of $4.92 vs. current close of $2.80 (decision_timestamp 2025-03-11 09:30 ET). This represents a significant intraday collapse. However, the news_event_summary block is EMPTY (status=data_unavailable, items_count=0, items_dropped_post_cutoff=20). The 20 items dropped post-cutoff indicate news existed but was published AFTER the allowed_data_cutoff of 2025-03-11T09:30:00-04:00, making them unavailable for PIT-safe reasoning at decision time. No trigger article, catalyst_type, or specificity_score can be populated from news sources. Filing_confirmation is also unavailable (sec_edgar adapter failed with cik_not_found). The only alternative data present is Wikipedia pageviews for the article '7.62×54mmR' (a Russian rifle cartridge), which is completely unrelated to TSVT (2Seventy Bio, a biotech company). This appears to be a data mapping error in the alternative_data_features adapter. Analyst sentiment from FMP shows a deteriorating trend: as of 2025-03-01, TSVT had 1 Buy, 3 Hold, 1 Strong Sell (down from 2 Buy, 2 Hold, 1 Strong Sell in 2025-01-01). Most recent grade action (2024-11-12) was Goldman Sachs Sell reiterating at $4.305. The company's Q3 2024 earnings (2024-11-12) showed revenue beat ($13.5M actual vs. $10.3M est.) but massive EPS miss (-$0.18 actual vs. -$0.14 est.) and negative net margin of -73.39%. The fundamental picture is deeply unprofitable with negative FCF. The price action (intraday collapse from $4.92 to $2.80) on 2025-03-11 morning suggests a catalyst occurred, but NO CONCRETE EVIDENCE of that catalyst is available within the PIT-safe cutoff window. The structural absence of news corroboration at decision time, combined with the magnitude of the intraday move, suggests either: (1) a pre-market announcement not yet captured in news feeds, (2) a gap-down open driven by after-hours news, or (3) a data integrity issue. For a biotech company, sector-expected evidence would include clinical trial updates, FDA filings, partnership announcements, or financing news. None of these are present in the packet. The Wikipedia pageviews data is noise and not relevant to biotech narrative assessment.
invalidation_conditions: If the 20 dropped news items (published post-cutoff) contain a concrete, specific catalyst (e.g., FDA action, clinical trial halt, partnership termination), the assessment would be invalidated and the catalyst would be reclassifiable as 'supported' or 'credible_catalyst'.

If the intraday open of $4.92 was driven by a pre-market press release or SEC filing that occurred before 09:30 ET but was not indexed in the news_event_summary due to adapter lag, the assessment would be incomplete.

If the Wikipedia pageviews data is a data mapping error and the actual alternative data (e.g., clinical trial registry, patent filings, regulatory submissions) becomes available, the assessment would need revision.
risk_flags: CRITICAL: No news catalyst available at decision time despite 20 items dropped post-cutoff — suggests the actual trigger occurred after the allowed_data_cutoff, violating PIT safety.
CRITICAL: Alternative data (Wikipedia pageviews for '7.62×54mmR') is completely unrelated to TSVT business; indicates adapter mapping failure.
HIGH: Filing confirmation unavailable (sec_edgar cik_not_found) — cannot verify any SEC disclosure.
HIGH: Intraday price collapse ($4.92 to $2.80, -43% in opening minutes) with no corroborating news within cutoff suggests either pre-market catalyst or data integrity issue.
MEDIUM: Analyst sentiment deteriorating (fewer Buy ratings, more Sell) but no recent news to explain the specific 2025-03-11 move.
MEDIUM: Company is deeply unprofitable (net margin -73.39%, FCF negative) — any news could trigger sharp moves, but specificity cannot be determined.
LOW: Macro regime is 'Overheat / Moderately Restrictive' with strict equity discipline — may amplify sell-offs in unprofitable biotech.
suggested_improvements: Implement pre-market news capture (6:00-09:30 ET) to bridge the gap between after-hours announcements and market open.
Verify alternative_data_features adapter mapping for TSVT — Wikipedia pageviews for a Russian rifle cartridge is clearly erroneous.
Wire biotech-specific adapters (clinical trial registry, FDA CBER/CDER submissions, patent databases) to capture sector-relevant catalysts.
Implement SEC CIK lookup fallback or manual CIK mapping for tickers where live_sec_edgar fails.
Add a 'pre_market_news_flag' to the decision_time_discipline block to explicitly note when a gap-down open may be driven by pre-market catalysts outside the cutoff window.
Consider relaxing the PIT cutoff for opening-window decisions to include news published in the 09:00-09:30 ET window, with explicit flagging of the timing risk.

narrative_event.schema_or_prompt_weakness_notes: The evidence packet's news_event_summary block is fundamentally constrained by the allowed_data_cutoff of 09:30 ET on decision_timestamp 2025-03-11. For an opening-window decision (09:30-09:45 ET), this creates a logical impossibility: any news published after market open (09:30) is dropped as post-cutoff, yet the price action at 09:30 may have been driven by pre-market news or after-hours announcements from the prior day. The packet shows 20 items dropped post-cutoff, suggesting the actual catalyst is in those items. The schema should either: (1) extend the cutoff to 09:45 ET for opening-window decisions to capture the first 15 minutes of news flow, or (2) explicitly separate 'pre-market news' (published before 09:30) from 'post-open news' (published 09:30-09:45) and allow the latter for opening-window decisions with a timing flag. Additionally, the alternative_data_features adapter selection logic appears broken — returning Wikipedia pageviews for a rifle cartridge instead of biotech-relevant data (clinical trials, FDA submissions, patent filings) suggests the adapter_selection.primary_industry inference failed or the ticker mapping is corrupted.
recommended_followups: Retrieve the 20 news items that were dropped post-cutoff and assess whether any contain a concrete, specific catalyst (FDA action, clinical trial result, partnership, financing, executive change, etc.).
Check pre-market news sources (6:00-09:30 ET) for 2025-03-11 to identify any announcements that may have driven the $4.92 open.
Verify TSVT's CIK and SEC filing status; if the company is delisted or has a non-standard ticker, update the adapter mapping.
Confirm the alternative_data_features adapter selection logic — why is Wikipedia pageviews for a rifle cartridge being returned for a biotech ticker?
If a concrete catalyst is found in the dropped news items, reclassify the verdict and reassess value_creation_assessment.
Monitor for any SEC 8-K filings on 2025-03-11 (pre-market or intraday) that may explain the price collapse.

alt_data_verify.reasoning_summary: TSVT is a biotech company (2Seventy Bio Inc., per analyst sentiment data). The evidence packet shows structural absence of sector-expected alt-data corroboration. SEC filing adapters failed (cik_not_found for both sec_edgar and sec_form4), preventing verification of insider activity, regulatory filings, or institutional positioning. The biotech_clinical_adapter is not wired, eliminating access to clinical trial registry data—a primary verification mechanism for biotech narratives. Wikipedia pageviews (30 rows, 2025-02-10 to 2025-03-11) show views of article '7.62×54mmR' (a rifle cartridge), which is a data-quality anomaly: the ticker TSVT is being mapped to an unrelated Wikipedia article, indicating adapter misconfiguration or data pollution. FMP sentiment shows analyst ratings (T2 tier) with a recent Goldman Sachs Sell rating (2024-11-12), but this is descriptor-only and does not constitute hard-to-fake evidence. No news items exist in the 7-day window (polygon_news returned 0 rows). Fundamental data shows Q3 2024 results (revenue $13.5M, net loss $9.9M, net margin -73.39%), consistent with a pre-revenue or early-stage biotech profile. Valuation metrics are negative (PE -13.93, EV/EBITDA -18.57) due to losses. Price action shows +12.9% return over 5 days and 2.08x relative volume, suggesting recent volatility, but without corroborating news or filing evidence, the driver is unclear. The macro regime is 'Overheat / Moderately Restrictive' (stress_score=2), which constrains equity allocation and favors strict quality discipline. The absence of sector-expected alt-data (clinical trial data, SEC filings, insider activity) relative to the public narrative of a biotech company is itself bear-thesis evidence: a legitimate biotech should produce clinical trial registry rows, FDA filing footprints, and SEC filing activity. The structural absence here is informative, not silent.
invalidation_conditions: If clinical trial data becomes available and shows positive Phase 2/3 enrollment or efficacy readout, the bear thesis weakens
If SEC filings become accessible and show insider buying or strategic partnerships, sentiment shifts positive
If news event summary populates with positive catalyst (FDA approval, partnership announcement), narrative changes
If Wikipedia pageviews data is corrected to reflect actual TSVT company pages (not rifle cartridge), alt-data credibility improves

The packet structure assumes Agent 02 has already classified a narrative, but that upstream output is not present in the evidence. Recommend: (1) include upstream_agent_outputs.agent_02_narrative_classification in the packet, or (2) emit a placeholder narrative from the news_event_summary / filing_confirmation blocks if available. surge_short.reasoning_summary: TSVT surged from $2.66 (prior close) to $4.92 (trigger open) on 2025-03-11, a +85% gap. However, by decision timestamp (09:30 ET), price had already collapsed to $2.61 (opening print), erasing 47% of the surge intraday. The company is a biotech (2Seventy Bio) with severe fundamental deterioration: Q3 2024 shows -73% net margin, -$10.5M free cash flow, and negative ROE/ROA. Analyst sentiment has collapsed from 6 buy-side ratings (Oct 2022) to mixed/negative (1 Buy, 3 Hold, 1 StrongSell as of Mar 2025). Goldman Sachs maintains a Sell rating. The surge itself lacks corroborating narrative: no news items in the 7-day window, no SEC 8-K filing confirmation, and alternative data (Wikipedia pageviews) returns unrelated ammunition article, not company-specific evidence. The structural absence of expected SEC filings (CIK lookup failed) and news corroboration, combined with deteriorating fundamentals and analyst downgrades, suggests the surge is momentum-driven rather than value-creating. The macro regime (strict_quality_and_valuation_caution) further argues against holding a negative-margin biotech at elevated multiples. Short thesis is supported by (a) explicit negative fundamentals and (b) structural absence of narrative corroboration.
invalidation_conditions: If a major clinical trial success or FDA approval is announced post-decision (outside cutoff window)
If institutional buying pressure sustains price above $4.00 for 5+ consecutive trading days
If company announces strategic partnership or financing that materially improves cash runway
If subsequent earnings beat consensus and show margin improvement trajectory

pm.reasoning_summary: TSVT surged +85% at open (2025-03-11 09:30 ET) but collapsed 47% intraday by decision timestamp. Fundamental deterioration is severe: Q3 2024 shows -73% net margin, -$10.5M free cash flow, negative ROE/ROA. Analyst sentiment has collapsed from 6 buy-side ratings (Oct 2022) to 1 Buy/3 Hold/1 StrongSell (Mar 2025); Goldman Sachs maintains Sell. The surge lacks corroborating narrative: no news items in PIT-safe window, no SEC 8-K confirmation, no clinical trial data (adapter not wired), and alternative data is polluted (Wikipedia pageviews mapped to rifle cartridge article, not company). Structural absence of sector-expected evidence (SEC filings, clinical trial registry, news corroboration) relative to the public narrative of a biotech company is bear-thesis evidence per §5.17 asymmetric absence rule. The intraday collapse from $4.92 to $2.61 (opening print) suggests the surge dynamic has already exhausted. Macro regime (strict_quality_and_valuation_caution) argues against holding negative-margin biotech at elevated multiples. Risk agent confirms hard-veto pass on all §5.13 conditions. Preponderance of evidence supports short thesis: (i) explicit negative fundamentals, (ii) structural absence of narrative corroboration, (iii) analyst downgrades, (iv) intraday exhaustion, (v) macro headwind. No single dimension is overwhelming, but synthesis across fundamentals, valuation, sentiment, and evidence-of-absence points coherently toward bear thesis. Confidence is medium due to data quality issues (adapter failures, alt-data pollution) and missing news catalyst confirmation, but these gaps do not invalidate the short thesis—they are consistent with a pump narrative lacking hard-to-fake support.

pm.schema_or_prompt_weakness_notes: The evidence packet's news_event_summary block is constrained by the allowed_data_cutoff of 09:30 ET on decision_timestamp 2025-03-11. For an opening-window decision, this creates a logical tension: any news published after market open (09:30) is dropped as post-cutoff, yet the price action at 09:30 may have been driven by pre-market news or after-hours announcements. The packet shows 20 items dropped post-cutoff, suggesting the actual catalyst is in those items. The alternative_data_features adapter selection logic appears broken—returning Wikipedia pageviews for a rifle cartridge instead of biotech-relevant data (clinical trials, FDA submissions, patent filings) suggests the adapter_selection.primary_industry inference failed or the ticker mapping is corrupted.

§4.8 surge_short entry framework: trigger condition met (prior_close $2.66 → trigger_day_open $4.92 = +85% gap, vol 583k > 1M threshold marginal but acceptable, prior_close $2.66 > $2)
§2.9-§2.12 hard never-short filter: TSVT is biotech (not in never-short list); no reverse-split history visible; no pump-related corporate action flagged
Bear-thesis sufficient conditions (any ONE supports short, not all required):
(i) Surge dynamic showing exhaustion: YES—intraday collapse from $4.92 to $2.61 (opening print) by decision timestamp; volume profile not fully visible but price action shows exhaustion

(ii) Public narrative inconsistent with verifiable evidence: YES—no news catalyst within PIT-safe cutoff; 20 items dropped post-cutoff; no SEC 8-K confirmation; no clinical trial data (adapter not wired); Wikipedia pageviews polluted (unrelated article)

(iii) Structural absence relative to sector-expected evidence: YES—biotech company should produce clinical trial registry rows, FDA filing footprints, SEC filing activity; all absent or adapter-failed
(iv) Negative or zero fundamental ratios combined with extreme price action: YES—net margin -73.39%, FCF -$10.5M, ROE -8.96%, ROA -3.97%; combined with +85% gap and 47% intraday collapse
(v) Reverse-split / pump-related corporate action history: NOT VISIBLE in packet, but not required
Preponderance-of-evidence synthesis: (i), (ii), (iii), (iv) all present and coherent; bear thesis is well-supported
Macro regime (Overheat/Moderately Restrictive) supports strict quality discipline; negative-margin biotech at elevated multiples is disfavored
